prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. Dining room of the same California suburban home, evening. The Mori family — four people — eating dinner together at the dining table. Kenji (father, 38, Japanese man, short hair) and Yuki (mother, 36, Japanese woman, semi-long hair) sit across from each other. Ren (son, 10, Japanese boy) and Hana (daughter, 7, Japanese girl with bright expression) sit on the other sides. On the table: salmon and summer salad — a colorful, healthy meal. Chopsticks and small dishes. They are in natural mid-conversation — Hana is saying something animated, Kenji is smiling, Yuki is reaching for a serving dish, Ren is eating. In the far background, the Samsung Family Hub screen glows dimly on the refrigerator — but nobody is looking at it. It's there but irrelevant now. Overhead dining light creates warm downward illumination. Last traces of sunset glow through the window. Color grading: warm evening tones, film-like with gentle desaturation. Food colors are natural and appetizing but not commercial-food-photography vivid. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. The dining table is shot from a diagonal angle, showing all four family members naturally. The Family Hub is a soft glow in the deep background. Less negative space than other shots — the family fills the frame with their togetherness. Camera: 50mm lens, f/2.0, ISO 400. Slightly more pronounced film grain for the evening warmth. Shallow depth with old-lens bokeh in the background. The mood is 'everything is handled, so we can just be together.' The invisible system working perfectly means the family's attention is fully on each other.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic skin, studio lighting, posed family portrait, stock photo, commercial food photography, dark moody, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, skin smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration, Western family
